name: Somerset Regal Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank founded in 1887 offering consumer and business online banking, mobile services, loans, and deposit products with local decision-making.
description_long: Somerset Regal Bank is a traditional bank established in 1887, operating with physical locations including a West Orange office. The bank maintains FDIC insurance backing all deposits with the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing customers with deposit protection up to regulatory limits. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking services centered around digital accessibility and convenience. Core offerings include consumer and business online banking (free enrollment), mobile banking for iOS and Android with remote deposit capture, a mobile wallet for contactless payments, savings accounts with competitive rates (3.35% APY on Premium Money Market accounts), certificates of deposit, home loans (5/1 ARM at 5.375% rate, 6.187% APR), and personal loans available through online application. Additional services include bill pay, eStatements, account alerts, free Visa check cards, Zelle transfers, check reorder, direct deposit, bank-by-phone access (800-420-1220), and free document shred events. Somerset Regal Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local decision-making for mortgage lending, free mortgage counseling for home loan applicants, and 24/7 digital access that contrasts with traditional "banker's hours." The bank has modernized its service delivery while maintaining a physical branch presence. The mobile wallet offering is promoted as having "hard to hack" security features with built-in protections and non-sharing of card numbers. The bank is positioned as a traditional community bank modernizing its digital capabilities. However, the website provides limited information about account minimums, fee structures (beyond noting Premium Money Market has no fees), loan approval criteria, or competitive positioning. The April 2026 maintenance window indicates ongoing system updates. For consumers seeking a full-service bank with both digital convenience and local branch access, Somerset Regal Bank presents a traditional option, though specific product details remain limited on their public website.
services: Mobile banking apps (iOS and Android with remote deposit capture)
Premium Money Market savings account (3.35% APY)
Account alerts and Zelle money transfers
Free Visa check card with check reorder service
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Free online banking, mobile banking apps (iOS/Android), and bank-by-phone service (800-420-1220)
Mobile wallet with contactless payment capability and built-in security features
3.35% APY Premium Money Market account with check-writing privileges and no fees
Home loans feature local decision-making and free mortgage counseling
Remote mobile deposit via photo capture on smartphone
Free Visa check card with mobile wallet compatibility
Founded in 1887 with established physical branch presence (West Orange office)
cons: Limited transparency on account minimums, monthly fees, and overdraft policies on homepage
No information provided about personal loan rates, terms, or approval criteria
Website lacks details on business banking account types and associated fees
Scheduled maintenance windows result in complete banking access unavailability
No mention of mobile wallet adoption rate, device compatibility limitations, or accepted payment networks beyond general statement

contact.address: 220 W Union Ave, Bound Brook, NJ 08805

best_for: Consumers seeking traditional bank with modern digital banking and local branch access
Homebuyers interested in 5/1 ARM mortgages with local underwriting and free counseling
Customers wanting FDIC-insured savings with competitive money market rates and check writing
Smartphone users wanting contactless mobile wallet payments with bank security backing
diagnosis: Somerset Regal Bank is best for consumers seeking a traditional community bank that has modernized its digital offerings, particularly those valuing FDIC protection, local branch presence, and 24/7 online/mobile access. The main caveat is that the public website lacks detailed information about account minimums, fee structures, and loan qualification criteria, requiring direct contact for specific product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
